articles.title: I tried to build a post scheduler but meta app review messed it up
articles.summary: I wanted to build something to help me post to different platforms, and I created the product. But yesterday, before launching it, I realized that right now, I'm currently in development mode for the authentication from Meta and for other people to use it, I need to publish th...
articles.content: I wanted to build something to help me post to different platforms, and I created the product. But yesterday, before launching it, I realized that right now, I'm currently in development mode for the authentication from Meta and for other people to use it, I need to publish the app. But While trying to publish the app, it asked me to verify my business, but I don't have a business and there were no options besides if you're a registered company/ corporate / sole proprietor etc all that legal info. And that made me realize that nobody besides me can use it because I'm the only one who has to add beta testers. and I have to do it manually, which means, you know, other people would not be able to use my app. And that was how meta messed it up for me. So i did what any other developer would do i made it open source and self hostable with docker where anyone can run it up for themselves with their env's. if you would like to check it out: https://github.com/anirudhprmar/post-mate

articles.title: Show HN: Fence – Jiminy Cricket for AI coding agents
articles.summary: Hi everyone, I'm Andrios, founder of hoop.dev (YC W21). 2 weeks ago, I told our eng team they could spend 20% of their week on side projects. The result: They came out with 3 open source projects. Tools we're using ourselves. Fence is the first one. It prevents an agent, confu...
articles.content:
Hi everyone, I'm Andrios, founder of hoop.dev (YC W21).

2 weeks ago, I told our eng team they could spend 20% of their week on side projects. The result: They came out with 3 open source projects. Tools we're using ourselves.

Fence is the first one. It prevents an agent, confused or poorly prompted, to execute a catastrophic command before it happens. This could be 'rm -rf~’, ‘rm -fr~’ or even ‘sudo rm -rf $HOME’. It’s different from a denylist, because it reads the intention, not only the string.

We use this for Claude Code and Codex. We want to see adoption before jumping to Cursor next.

The team is very excited to release this, so please to drop any comments with feedback or issues you find:

github.com/hoophq/fence

articles.title: News24 | eThekwini approves pay hike: Here’s what municipal workers will get
articles.summary: The eThekwini Municipality has approved and implemented a 4.75% salary and wage increase for municipal employees, effective 1 July, in line with the South African Local Government Bargaining Council’s collective agreement.
articles.content:
• eThekwini Municipality has approved a 4.75% salary and wage increase for municipal employees, effective 1 July.
  • IFP councillor Ntombifuthi Ntshayintshayi cautioned that increases must be sufficient to make a real difference for workers amid rising costs.
  • The approval follows a December 2025 benchmarking settlement between the municipality and the SA Municipal Workers’ Union in eThekwini.

The eThekwini Municipality has approved and implemented a 4.75% salary and wage increase for municipal employees, effective 1 July, in line with the South African Local Government Bargaining Council’s (SALGBC) collective agreement.

This comes as the municipality also moves to increase its draft budget for the new financial year. In May, News24 reported that eThekwini Mayor Cyril Xaba had tabled a R75.5 billion budget.

The salary increase recommendation was adopted during a full council meeting at the Inkosi Albert Luthuli International Convention Centre in Durban, KwaZulu-Natal, on Tuesday.

Initially, the full council meeting was scheduled to take place on 30 June, but due to nationwide protests by the anti-immigration group March and March, it was postponed to 7 July.

A report, tabled by the governance and human resources committee on 11 June, stated that on 13 March, the bargaining council issued a circular confirming the salary and wage adjustment for the 2026/27 financial year.

The agreement covers the financial years 2024/25 to 2028/29 and is currently being implemented.

  • General salary and wage increase: 4.75%.
  • All linked benefits and conditions of service: 4.75% adjustment.
  • Minimum wage increased from R10 616 to R11 120.
  • Homeowners’ allowance increased from R1 170 to R1 225.
  • The maximum employer medical aid contribution increased from R5 791 to R6 066.

The DA abstained from the matter due to its legal obligations regarding the salary and wage increase under the collective agreement.

The committee recommended that the report on the salary and wage increase be noted.

While most councillors agreed on the salary and wage increase, IFP councillor Ntombifuthi Ntshayintshayi raised a point of order.

She said: “The IFP supports this item. We understand that the current economic situation demands that salaries help people earn a decent living. However, we want to caution that when we increase salaries, we must consider whether those increases genuinely improve the lives of our employees in the current economic climate.”

Ntshayintshayi said the municipality must not increase salaries for the sake of it.

She added:

Regarding the financial implications, the municipality said it has made provision for the increase in the draft budget for the 2026/2027 financial year.

In December 2025, IOL reported that the South African Municipal Workers’ Union (Samwu) in eThekwini announced a landmark settlement agreement on benchmarking, following sustained union advocacy to address pay parity, among other issues.

READ | eThekwini reduces tariff hikes after public outcry

The agreement means that workers would be paid the same as their counterparts in other metros across the country.

The agreement was reportedly set to cost the municipality an additional R500 million a year. To offset that expense, the city reportedly proposed reducing overtime and introducing a shift system.

The union and the municipality had reportedly been locked in a prolonged dispute over pay disparities between eThekwini and other municipalities. Workers had reportedly previously complained that they were being paid significantly less than their counterparts in other metros.

articles.title: I have an interesting idea to reduce the output tokens almost by fifty percent
articles.summary: Most token-saving tools focus on input tokens, but output tokens are usually priced higher, and edit-heavy agent sessions waste a lot of them on a specific pattern: to safely edit a file, the agent has to output the old code block plus the new code block, just so the harness c...
articles.content:
Most token-saving tools focus on input tokens, but output tokens are usually priced higher, and edit-heavy agent sessions waste a lot of them on a specific pattern: to safely edit a file, the agent has to output the old code block plus the new code block, just so the harness can match and swap it in. That's safe (content-based matching means line drift doesn't cause a bad edit), but it means half the tokens going out are just the agent restating content it already read.

Plain line-range edits ("replace lines 50-60") avoid the resend but aren't safe, the moment any prior edit shifts the file, the range is stale and the agent edits the wrong lines.

My proposed fix keeps content-based safety without the resend:

1. On file read, return numbered lines + a blob id for that exact snapshot. 2. On edit, the agent sends only the blob id, a line range from that snapshot, and the new content. No old code required. 3. The system finds that range in the stored blob (not the live file), applies the change there, and three-way merges it into the current file (git's merge machinery handles this well).

Since the anchor is an immutable snapshot rather than the live file, the agent is replaying a known edit against a known point-in-time, and letting the merge reconcile it with whatever the file looks like now. Net effect: only new content is emitted as output tokens, instead of new + old.

Caveat: I built a CLI proof of concept, and it works on small files, but now I found most agent harnesses truncate bash tool output around 16KB, so round-tripping file reads through a bash channel breaks down on anything bigger. That's a limitation of doing this externally, implemented natively inside a harness (where reads/edits don't have to go through a size-capped channel), it shouldn't apply.

Repo (mechanics reference, not production-ready): https://github.com/Brajesh2022/VCS-edit-tools

My attempt to fix it failed because of the truncation limitations. I hope any harness builder who sees this post will apply it to a harness.

articles.title: The Business of Benefits: Onsite childcare at J.M. Smucker
articles.summary: J.M. Smucker Co. has provided onsite childcare for its employees since 2014, operating a child development center at its Ohio headquarters for infants through preschoolers, with a summer camp for older children. The company views this benefit as a talent differentiator, offeri...
articles.content:
Here’s this week’s edition of our Business of Benefits series, where we spotlight benefits that are driving value for HR leaders, digging into their design and ROI.

If you’re interested in talking about a workplace benefit you love for an upcoming edition, click here to get in touch. You can also reach out directly to HR Brew’s total rewards reporter, Courtney Vinopal: [email protected]

Since 2014, the food manufacturing company J.M. Smucker Co. has offered onsite childcare for its employees’ children and grandchildren. The child development center, which is run by Bright Horizons, is located at the company’s Orrville, Ohio headquarters, and serves children from infancy through preschool. A summer camp program is also available to children aged 6–12.

Smucker has previously said it sees the benefit as a differentiator when attracting talent. Having childcare onsite allows employees to stay connected with their children to nurse during the workday, for example, or visit the class to read. Hind Simonetti, VP of talent, told us more about what success looks like, more than 10 years on.

Explain the business case for this benefit.

We are extremely proud to have cultivated a workplace that supports our employees holistically. This means truly understanding their needs and identifying meaningful solutions. In considering the needs of working parents, we acknowledged the challenge of finding affordable childcare. According to recent research, almost half of parents with children five and younger believe childcare is difficult to find.

The Child Development Center gives us an opportunity to support this need and create greater satisfaction for our employees, ultimately supporting our ability to attract and retain top talent.

Quick-to-read HR news & insights

From recruiting and retention to company culture and the latest in HR tech, HR Brew delivers up-to-date industry news and tips to help HR pros stay nimble in today’s fast-changing business environment.

What level of investment does this benefit represent for your organization?

While we wouldn’t provide specifics of the cost structure/investment, we can share that the subsidization gives parents first-rate care for their children at a market rate.

How are you measuring the ROI of this benefit?

Overall, the most important thing to us is that the center supports enhanced employee satisfaction and that has come to life in several ways. In addition to the important peace of mind the center provides, there are unique opportunities it affords our employees.

  • Opportunities for employees to bring their children to campus for events such as trick-or-treat, outdoor games, and live entertainment.
  • The ability for employees to go to the center for events such as festivals, open houses, reading to a class, and utilizing the nursing mother’s room.
  • Increased flexibility including full-time and part-time schedules for our hybrid work environment and a drop-in care benefit, which does not require enrollment and can accommodate employees who travel in for our core weeks.
  • Reliability when other childcare falls through or there are school closures.

Parents have expressed tremendous appreciation for this resource on campus. To offer context, in this year’s family satisfaction survey, 100% of respondents affirmed:

  • Satisfaction with the care and education their children receive
  • That access to the center positively impacts their ability to concentrate on their job and be productive
  • The center reduces the amount of stress faced in managing personal and professional demands

Quick-to-read HR news & insights

From recruiting and retention to company culture and the latest in HR tech, HR Brew delivers up-to-date industry news and tips to help HR pros stay nimble in today’s fast-changing business environment.

articles.title: Show HN: FlowHook – a file watcher that knows if your build passed or failed
articles.summary: FlowHook is a CLI tool that watches your files and executes given commands, similar to watchexec. But I believe it has three things that differentiate it from existing tools. It persists your configuration. If you set up flowhook instance in your folder and add the necessary c...
articles.content:
FlowHook is a CLI tool that watches your files and executes given commands, similar to watchexec. But I believe it has three things that differentiate it from existing tools.

It persists your configuration. If you set up flowhook instance in your folder and add the necessary commands you only need to run `flowhook run` to start watching anytime.

It logs your build output plus other info (timestamp, exit code, etc.) to a JSON log file inside your project. Use cases include analyzing build history, debugging, etc. Personally I'm excited about this one. This was even the thing that convinced me to take this project seriously. Tools that analyze your success/failure ratio, most common time of build failures, and similar patterns could be built on top of this using the JSON data.

Third it lets you hook on-success and on-failure commands. These are separate commands that run based on the exit code of your base command. Use cases include running a test suite on success, a desktop notification on failure, committing to git on success, kicking off an agent to analyze the error on failure, etc.

TL;DR : FlowHook is a CLI tool that watches your file and execute given commands(like watchexec), but it also allows you to hook on-success and on-failure commands. It also logs your build output to a JSON log file in your project directory.

Excited about where this can go and would love your feedback.

articles.title: Cramer’s Take: GEV, CAT, INTC Have Room—Today Is A Punisher - Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT)
articles.summary: Jim Cramer commented on GE Vernova (GEV), Caterpillar (CAT), and Intel (INTC), asserting that despite recent market volatility causing their stock prices to drop, their fundamental bull cases remain intact. He believes that the current sell-off is a short-term rotation of capi...
articles.content:
CNBC host Jim Cramer commented on some of the market’s hottest stocks on Tuesday, noting that structurally intact bull cases are facing a vicious short‑term rotation.

  • INTC stock is down today. See the chart and price action here.

‘No Top, Just A Brutal Rotation’

Cramer is signaling that despite Tuesday’s painful volatility, the investment case in INTC, GEV and CAT is not broken.

For GE Vernova, the thesis centers on the multi‑year buildout of energy infrastructure and the modernization of the grid. The market has rewarded that story, and Cramer’s "no top" phrasing suggests belief that earnings power and backlog support higher valuations over time.

The brutal rotation, however, can knock down even quality industrials when capital moves abruptly between AI-anchored tech, defensive staples and cyclicals.

According to Benzinga Pro data, GE Vernova stock was down 8.84% at $1,050.81 on Tuesday afternoon.

Caterpillar fits a similar pattern. The bull case hinges on infrastructure spending, bolstered by the breakneck pace of AI-related buildouts.

Cramer’s point is that such selling does not necessarily mark a fundamental peak. Instead, it reflects a market that is repricing sectors in a hurry, leaving holders with drawdowns that feel worse than the underlying business trajectory would justify.

Caterpillar stock was down 5.31% at $918.43 on Tuesday afternoon, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Intel is a bit different in that the narrative has been more contested, but the core idea remains: a multi‑year turnaround in manufacturing, government‑backed domestic capacity and participation in AI and high‑performance computing.

Short‑term rotation inside semiconductors, especially when investors crowd into perceived AI winners, can slam legacy chip names even as their strategic positioning improves.

Intel stock was down 9.32% at $110.82 on Tuesday afternoon, according to Benzinga Pro data.

The Bottom Line

For longer‑horizon investors, Cramer’s take is a reminder to separate price action driven by rotation from actual deterioration in fundamentals.

In his framework, GEV, CAT, and INTC still have room to run; the current selloff is a test of conviction rather than a signal that the bull cases have ended.

This image was generated using artificial intelligence via Gemini.

articles.title: 5 Exclusive iPad Features You Won't Find On An Android Tablet
articles.summary: This article highlights five exclusive features of the iPad that differentiate it from Android tablets: Universal Control, Math Notes, Smart Script, a wide selection of exclusive apps, and Sidecar. These features leverage Apple's integrated software and hardware ecosystem to p...
articles.content: This article highlights five exclusive features of the iPad that differentiate it from Android tablets: Universal Control, Math Notes, Smart Script, a wide selection of exclusive apps, and Sidecar. These features leverage Apple's integrated software and hardware ecosystem to provide unique functionalities like seamless control across multiple Apple devices, intelligent handwriting recognition for math and general note-taking, and transforming an iPad into a high-performance external monitor for a Mac. The author contends that while Android tablets are improving, the iPad's exclusive offerings still provide a superior and more consistent user experience, especially for specific tasks.

articles.title: Old Dominion Freight Line focuses on core operations as retail demand shapes outlook
articles.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line is maintaining its focus on disciplined operations, customer service, and efficiency in its less-than-truckload business, adapting to evolving US retail demand and freight patterns. The company emphasizes a strong network, cost management, and service...
articles.content:
Old Dominion Freight Line (ISIN US6826801036) is a major player in the US less-than-truckload market, known for combining nationwide coverage with a focus on service quality and operating efficiency. The company’s network and disciplined approach to pricing and costs remain central as freight demand tracks broader US consumer and industrial activity.

Operational discipline in LTL freight

Old Dominion Freight Line operates a dense network of service centers and linehaul routes that allow it to consolidate smaller shipments into efficient loads. This less-than-truckload structure means shippers can move freight without filling an entire trailer, a model that depends heavily on terminal productivity, routing optimization, and reliable transit times.

The company emphasizes on-time performance and low damage rates, making service metrics a differentiator in a segment where many customers value reliability over the lowest possible rate. Tight control of pickup and delivery operations, careful planning of long-haul schedules, and investment in technology help support consistent service in a cyclical freight environment.

Cost management and pricing strategy

For Old Dominion Freight Line, managing costs per shipment is critical to maintaining margins when volumes fluctuate. Fuel, labor, equipment, and maintenance are among the largest expense categories, and the company works continuously to align them with demand and mix. Network planning seeks to minimize empty miles and balance capacity across regions, reducing waste and improving asset utilization.

Pricing decisions in less-than-truckload freight reflect both underlying cost trends and the competitive landscape. Old Dominion Freight Line typically aims to secure freight that fits its network and service profile, rather than pursuing volume at the expense of profitability. General rate increases, lane-specific adjustments, and contractual negotiations are tools to keep yields aligned with service levels and cost inflation.

Business model and customer mix

Old Dominion Freight Line’s business model is built around serving a broad base of small, medium, and large shippers that value predictable transit and consistent handling of freight. Customers come from sectors such as manufacturing, retail, and distribution, each with distinct seasonality and shipment profiles. This diversification helps smooth demand over the year, as strength in one area can offset weakness in another.

The company’s long-haul orientation means it often carries freight over extended distances across multiple regions, connecting local pickup and delivery operations through its linehaul network. Standardized processes across terminals and consistent service policies make it easier to provide similar experiences to customers in different parts of the country, supporting brand recognition in a fragmented industry.

Technology and terminal infrastructure

Efficient less-than-truckload operations rely on information flow as much as on physical assets. Old Dominion Freight Line uses routing and dispatch systems to manage trailer loading, linehaul departures, and local delivery schedules, aiming to reduce delays and optimize capacity. Shipment tracking and electronic documentation also support transparency for customers and internal control over freight movements.

Terminal infrastructure is another key part of the model. Cross-dock facilities must handle incoming and outgoing shipments quickly while minimizing handling damage. Layout decisions, dock door configuration, and material-handling equipment all contribute to throughput, and incremental improvements can compound into meaningful productivity gains over time.

Service quality as a competitive factor

Service quality is central to Old Dominion Freight Line’s positioning. Many shippers are willing to pay a premium for fewer claims, more accurate transit times, and responsive customer support. By focusing on execution at each step of the shipment path, the company seeks to reduce variability and build trust with repeat customers.

Low claims ratios and strong on-time performance also help lower internal costs associated with rework and dispute resolution. This combination of external reputation and internal savings can make service quality an economic advantage rather than just a marketing statement, especially in a market where competitors differ widely in reliability.

Representative service: long-haul LTL offering

A representative element of Old Dominion Freight Line’s business is its long-haul less-than-truckload service, where freight moves between distant regions through a series of linehaul and terminal stops. Customers ship pallets or partial loads that are consolidated with other freight, benefiting from the network’s scale without needing to book full-truckload capacity. Consistent transit times, proactive communication, and careful freight handling are core features of this offering.

Stock context and trading venue

Old Dominion Freight Line stock trades in the United States, reflecting investor interest in the less-than-truckload segment and broader transportation sector. The listing connects the company’s operational performance and strategic decisions with equity-market expectations, where factors such as freight demand, cost trends, and capital allocation influence sentiment over time.

This article was generated automatically and technically reviewed before publication. Market prices, analyst data and company information are provided without warranty and may change at short notice. articles.title: Equinix Inc balances global data center growth and investor expectations
articles.summary: Equinix Inc operates a vast global network of colocation and interconnection data centers, serving as a critical infrastructure provider for enterprise and cloud customers. The company's business model relies on recurring revenue from long-term contracts and continuous investm...
articles.content:
Equinix Inc (ISIN US29476L1070) runs one of the largest globally interconnected data center platforms, providing colocation space, power and connectivity for enterprise, cloud and network customers who rely on its facilities to host critical digital infrastructure.

As a major operator in an industry that serves hyperscale cloud providers, financial institutions, content platforms and telecom carriers, the company’s business model combines recurring revenue from long-term contracts with ongoing investment in new capacity and technology upgrades in existing sites.

Global data center footprint and interconnection focus

The company operates data centers across key metropolitan areas in North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific and other regions, giving customers the ability to place equipment near end users and critical network hubs while benefiting from consistent operational standards across campuses.

Within those facilities, customers lease secure cages, racks and power capacity, and can purchase additional managed services such as remote hands support, cross-connects between their equipment and other tenants, and access to network fabrics that simplify complex routing requirements.

A core element of Equinix Inc’s strategy is interconnection, which allows different organizations to connect directly to each other’s infrastructure within the same building or campus rather than traversing the public internet, often improving performance, reliability and security for latency-sensitive applications.

The business has evolved beyond traditional colocation by offering software-defined connectivity, allowing enterprises and service providers to dynamically adjust bandwidth and routing between sites or external cloud platforms without physically rewiring connections.

Capital investment, competition and customer mix

Operating and expanding a large data center portfolio requires substantial capital expenditure on land, buildings, electrical systems, cooling infrastructure and backup power, as well as continuous upgrades to maintain energy efficiency and resilience standards demanded by customers.

Management teams in this sector typically weigh the timing and scale of expansion projects against expected demand from existing and potential customers, seeking to maintain high utilization of installed capacity without constraining growth in markets where digital traffic and cloud usage are rising.

The company faces competition from other data center operators, cloud providers that build their own infrastructure, and regional players that focus on specific metropolitan areas or customer segments, making differentiation through network density, ecosystem breadth and service quality an important part of its positioning.

Customer relationships often span many years, with decisions about where to locate equipment influenced by technical requirements, regulatory considerations, data sovereignty rules and total cost of ownership calculations that factor in power prices, connectivity costs and operational risk.

Representative interconnection and colocation services

One representative pillar of Equinix Inc’s offering is standardized retail colocation, where customers deploy individual racks or small footprints in shared data halls while still benefiting from robust physical security, redundant power and access to a wide range of carrier and cloud connectivity options.

Alongside that, larger enterprises and service providers can contract for multi-rack suites or bespoke configurations that support high-density computing, specialized cooling or compliance with specific industry standards, such as financial services requirements for resilience and record retention.

Interconnection services enable direct links between customers, network providers and cloud platforms inside facilities, helping organizations optimize application performance, reduce bandwidth costs and establish secure architectures for hybrid and multi-cloud deployments.

Software-driven network fabrics sit on top of this physical connectivity, giving customers more flexibility to orchestrate traffic flows between sites, clouds and partners while retaining visibility into usage and performance metrics for capacity planning and governance.

Stock trading context and investor perspective

Equinix Inc is listed in the United States and its shares represent exposure to the data center and digital infrastructure segment, which is closely watched by investors who track trends in cloud adoption, enterprise IT spending and network traffic volumes.

Investors often assess metrics such as revenue growth, recurring contract value, data center utilization, margin development and capital expenditure plans when forming opinions about companies in this space, alongside broader considerations like interest rates and financing conditions that affect infrastructure valuations.

articles.title: Brentford sign forward Anthony from Burnley
articles.summary: July 7 : Brentford have signed forward Jaidon Anthony from Burnley on a four-year contract with an option for a further year, the Premier League club said on Tuesday. The 26-year-old joins for an undisclosed fee after scoring nine goals in 38 appearances last season across all...
articles.content:
July 7 : Brentford have signed forward Jaidon Anthony from Burnley on a four-year contract with an option for a further year, the Premier League club said on Tuesday.

The 26-year-old joins for an undisclosed fee after scoring nine goals in 38 appearances last season across all competitions at Burnley, who were relegated to the second-tier Championship.

"We've been aware of Jaidon's journey for a long time and have always admired him from afar," said Brentford head coach Keith Andrews,

"He did really well last season, which is difficult in a team that got relegated."

Anthony made 81 appearances in all competitions for Burnley across two seasons, having initially joined on loan from Bournemouth before completing a permanent move last summer.

Brentford begin their 2026-27 Premier League campaign against Tottenham Hotspur on August 22.

articles.title: Swedbank AB Acquires 316,859 Shares of MetLife, Inc. $MET
articles.summary: Swedbank AB increased its stake in MetLife, Inc. by 13.3% in the first quarter, purchasing an additional 316,859 shares, bringing its total to 2.7 million shares valued at approximately $191 million. This comes as MetLife reported better-than-expected earnings of $2.42 per sha...
articles.content:
Swedbank AB grew its stake in shares of MetLife, Inc. (NYSE:MET - Free Report) by 13.3% in the first quarter, according to its most recent filing with the SEC. The institutional investor owned 2,702,087 shares of the financial services provider's stock after purchasing an additional 316,859 shares during the period. Swedbank AB owned about 0.41% of MetLife worth $191,092,000 as of its most recent filing with the SEC.

A number of other large investors also recently modified their holdings of MET. Unison Advisors LLC lifted its position in shares of MetLife by 0.7% during the first quarter. Unison Advisors LLC now owns 53,952 shares of the financial services provider's stock valued at $3,815,000 after purchasing an additional 387 shares in the last quarter. Kathmere Capital Management LLC grew its position in MetLife by 2.4% in the 1st quarter. Kathmere Capital Management LLC now owns 5,813 shares of the financial services provider's stock worth $411,000 after purchasing an additional 136 shares in the last quarter. Bogart Wealth LLC grew its position in MetLife by 36.4% in the 1st quarter. Bogart Wealth LLC now owns 941 shares of the financial services provider's stock worth $67,000 after purchasing an additional 251 shares in the last quarter. DGS Capital Management LLC increased its stake in MetLife by 25.7% in the 1st quarter. DGS Capital Management LLC now owns 5,949 shares of the financial services provider's stock valued at $421,000 after buying an additional 1,216 shares during the last quarter. articles.title: Show HN: Book Bounce – Bedtime story helper for parents
articles.summary: I built a helper for my son's story times. After a few months of reading to our son we couldn't remember what books we read and when, we realized that our personal library had hotspots, and we were going to need the option for variety when the time came to choose bedtime books...
articles.content:
I built a helper for my son's story times.

After a few months of reading to our son we couldn't remember what books we read and when, we realized that our personal library had hotspots, and we were going to need the option for variety when the time came to choose bedtime books.

This app gives my wife and I a rotation for our physical books based on the last time they were read and the frequency setting on each book. It also throws in other children's books on OpenLibrary to read to him. We've found some new favorites from this feature. Reading activity is logged as well. We've used it to track his 1000 Books Before Kindergarten progress.

Supabase stores a pool of OpenLibrary ebooks to randomly suggest to users. OpenLibrary's API is used as a book data source. Everything else stays local to your device.

TLDR: App that helps parents rotate through story books, suggests ebooks on OpenLibrary, tracks reading activity, data stays local.

articles.title: If You'd Invested $10,000 in Costco 10 Years Ago, Here's How Much You'd Have Today
articles.summary: An investment of $10,000 in Costco stock 10 years ago, with reinvested dividends, would now be worth approximately $72,000, representing an average annual return of about 22%. This significant growth was driven by Costco's robust membership-based business model and a rerating...
articles.content:
Ten years ago, Costco Wholesale (COST 0.30%) traded around $155 a share. If you had put $10,000 into the membership-based retailer back then and reinvested every dividend along the way, you would be sitting on about $72,000 today. That is more than seven times your money, or an average of about 22% a year -- the kind of compounding most investors only dream about, and it came from a warehouse-club stock.

So, what produced that result? And can the next decade for Costco stock come anywhere close?

The compounding engine

Costco's returns don't come from a flashy product cycle. They come from a business model that quietly compounds, and the heart of it is the membership fee. Customers pay an annual fee simply for the right to shop, which hands Costco a stream of high-margin, recurring income and a powerful incentive to keep prices low so members feel they are getting their money's worth.

The business model ultimately creates a flywheel. Low prices drive traffic and loyalty, loyalty drives membership renewals, and renewals help fund still-lower prices.

Further, Costco's renewal rates sit around 90%, and the model has proved remarkably durable across economic cycles.

And the growth hasn't slowed. In its fiscal third quarter (the period ended May 10, 2026), Costco's net sales rose 11.6% year over year to $69.2 billion. Its monthly figures have run even hotter lately. For the retail month of May, net sales climbed 14.5%, with comparable sales -- a measure of revenue at locations open at least a year -- up 12.5%, and digitally enabled comparable sales up 21.1%. For a retailer this size to still post double-digit growth, well into its fifth decade as a public company, is impressive.

And there is still room to grow. Costco keeps opening new warehouses every year, and the bulk of its locations remain in North America, leaving markets across Asia and Europe relatively underpenetrated. Earnings are compounding alongside sales -- net income rose about 10% in fiscal 2025 -- and the membership fee gives the company a lever it can pull every few years. The debate around the stock isn't about whether Costco keeps growing. It is about the price you pay to own that growth.

Key Data Points

Can it happen again?

The hard part for anyone buying today is understanding what actually drove those returns. That 22%-a-year gain came partly from Costco's growth and partly from investors, over the decade, deciding to pay an ever-higher price for that growth. Ten years ago, the stock traded at a price-to-earnings ratio in the high 20s. Today, it fetches about 48 times earnings.

That rerating did a lot of the heavy lifting, and it can't repeat forever. For the stock to return 22% a year again, investors a decade from now would have to pay something like 70 or 80 times earnings on top of continued strong growth. That is a stretch, and it is the crux of the problem with buying at today's price.

None of which makes Costco a bad business. It is one of the best retailers on the planet, membership renewals give it unusual visibility into future revenue, and at about 13% below its 52-week high, the stock isn't quite as pricey as it was a few months ago. The risk isn't that the business breaks. It is that a stock priced at 48 times earnings has almost no cushion, so even a modest slowdown -- a soft patch in comparable sales, or sharper competition from Amazon or Walmart's Sam's Club -- could pull the valuation multiple down even as the company keeps performing.

But at nearly 48 times earnings, I don't think today's buyers should expect a repeat of the last decade. Costco will very likely keep growing, keep nudging up its membership fee, and keep rewarding patient shareholders -- just probably at a more ordinary pace from this starting valuation. If you already own it, that is a fine reason to hold on. If you are looking to buy, history suggests the returns tend to be far better when you catch this stock on a meaningful pullback. And 13% off the high isn't quite that. Personally, I would keep it on my watch list and wait for a better price.
